_comment: Shared golden vectors for the re-auth threshold. Loaded by BOTH suites:
AppLockVectorTest.kt drives domain/AppLock.kt. The arithmetic is identical
down to the boundary — 'exactly at the threshold' prompts (>=) on both
platforms since the 0.83.0 QA round aligned Android's former strict >.
The monotonic source is elapsedRealtime on Android and a ContinuousClock
uptime on iOS; both keep counting while the device sleeps. All times in
SECONDS. thresholdSeconds is 30 on both platforms; it is repeated here so
a vector is self-contained and a change to the constant on one side shows
up as a vector mismatch on the other. backgroundedAt null means the app
never left the foreground this session.

requiresReauth.description: backgrounded a moment ago: no prompt
one second under the threshold: no prompt
a backwards reading counts as no elapsed time: no prompt
